nodes.en: Religious Studies investigates religions—their doctrines, practices, institutions, and social impacts—across cultures and history using humanistic and social-scientific methods. It encompasses theology (doctrine and belief), ritual and worship, scripture and myth, religious experience and mysticism, institutional history (churches, monasteries, hierarchies), and religion's roles in politics, ethics, and identity. Distinguished from Theology (which advocates within a tradition) by its analytical stance; it illuminates human meaning-making and cultural diversity.
nodes.cs: Religiologické studie zkoumají náboženství—jejich nauky, praktiky, instituce a sociální dopady—přes kultury a historii pomocí humanitních a sociálně-vědeckých metod. Zahrnují teologii (doktrínu a víru), rituál a uctívání, písmo a mýtus, náboženskou zkušenost a mystiku, institucionální historii (kostely, kláštery, hierarchie) a role náboženství v politice, etice a identitě. Rozlišují se od Teologie (která se zastává v tradici) svou analytickou pozicí; osvětlují lidské vytváření smyslu a kulturní rozmanitost.
nodes.brief:
Religious Studies investigates religions — their doctrines, practices, institutions, and social impacts — across cultures and history, using humanistic and social-scientific methods rather than doctrinal advocacy. It encompasses theology, ritual and worship, scripture and myth, religious experience and mysticism, and institutional history.

Within [[05]] Humanities, Religious Studies shares its analytical, argument-driven approach to belief with [[05.02]] Philosophy (philosophy of religion) while examining institutions and social impact alongside [[05.01]] History and [[04]] Social Sciences. It overlaps with [[10]] Cultural Preservation wherever scripture, ritual, and oral doctrine survive as endangered cultural heritage rather than living practice.

Distinguished from theology, which advocates within a tradition, Religious Studies illuminates how belief systems shape — and are shaped by — human meaning-making and cultural diversity. For comparative scholarship and professional standards, see the [American Academy of Religion](https://www.aarweb.org/); for primary religious texts across traditions, the [Internet Sacred Text Archive](https://sacred-texts.com/).
